title: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi
tagline: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi — spiritual town of Tamil Nadu

heroImage.alt: Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi, Tamil Nadu

overview.short: Kasi Viswanathar Temple in Sivakasi, a town in Virudhunagar district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva.
overview.description: Kasi Viswanathar Temple in Sivakasi, a town in Virudhunagar district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Shiva. Constructed in the South Indian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built by Pandyan ruler Harikesari Parakkirama Pandian during the 16th century, with later additions from Madurai Nayaks. Shiva is worshipped as Kasi Viswanathar and his consort Parvathi as Visalakshi. The temple was the scene of temple entry movement in 1899, followed by Sivakasi riots when 22 people were killed.

topPlaces.name: Badrakali Amman temple, Sivakasi
topPlaces.description: Badrakali Amman Temple in Sivakasi, a town in Virudhunagar district in the South Indian state of Tamil Nadu, is dedicated to the Hindu god Badrakali. Constructed in the Dravidian style of architecture, the temple is believed to have been built during the 18th century with later expansion during the 19th and 20th centuries.

topPlaces.name: Sivakasi
topPlaces.description: Sivakasi (also pronounced Shivakaashi) is a city in Virudhunagar District in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. The city is known for firecrackers and match factories that produce 70% of the country's output.

topPlaces.name: Viswanatham
topPlaces.description: Viswanatham is a panchayat town in Virudhunagar district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Thiruthangal
topPlaces.description: Thiruthangal is a neighbourhood of Sivakasi City, India.Thiruthangal is one of the Zones of Sivakasi City Municipal Corporation. Ninra Narayana Perumal temple, one among the 108 Divya Desams, Vishnu temples revered by saint poets, Alwars of 6th–9th century is located in Thiruthangal.

topPlaces.name: Anaiyur, Virudhunagar
topPlaces.description: Anaiyur is a part of Sivakasi city. It was once a panchayat town in Sivakasi block of Virudhunagar district.

topPlaces.name: Pallapatti, Virudhunagar
topPlaces.description: Pallapatti is a panchayat town in Virudhunagar district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Sithurajapuram
topPlaces.description: Sithurajapuram is an area of Sivakasi Corporation in Virudhunagar district in the Indian state of Tamil Nadu. There is a phc in sithurajapuram run by the government of Tamil Nadu.

topPlaces.name: Karunellinathar temple
topPlaces.description: Karunellinathar temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva in Thiruthangal, a town in the outskirts of Sivakasi, in Tamil Nadu, India. Shiva is worshiped as Karunellinathar, and is represented by the lingam and his consort Parvati is depicted as Chokki Amman.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Madurai Airport (54 km) and the nearest railway station is Madurai Junction (63 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi?
faq.a: Stays in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kasi Viswanathar temple, Sivakasi?
faq.a: The top attractions are Badrakali Amman temple, Sivakasi, Sivakasi, Viswanatham, Thiruthangal, Anaiyur, Virudhunagar.
